mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates accelerating labor market absorption, which raises aggregate demand expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in >55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint amplifies the equity risk premium compression effect from forward guidance, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as liquidity expectations remain anchored.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling unemployment and rising industrial production captures a non-linear virtuous cycle where labor market tightening coincides with supply-side capacity expansion, jointly forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond either series alone.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is falling and INDPRO is rising, the joint signal does not outperform the single best proxy by at least 3 percentage points in hit rate over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During housing recovery phases, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal durable goods demand and construction employment gains, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; this relationship weakens outside of housing-led recoveries.
failure_condition: During housing recovery regimes, HOUST increases are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of cases over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving manufacturing profitability and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month INDPRO growth are not positive in >55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail and food services sales below the 3-month trailing average signals consumer demand weakening that compresses revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change < 0 and below 3-month average change
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS surprises are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in <55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When rising consumer prices coincide with a low federal funds rate, negative real interest rates force capital out of fixed income into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this effect is stronger than either inflation or rates alone.
failure_condition: Joint positive inflation and low rate periods do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term is not significant at p<0.05 in a logistic regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above its 4-quarter average signals demand momentum that outpaces trend, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this signal is only reliable when the economy is outside of recession.
failure_condition: During non-recession expansions, above-trend GDP growth is not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >58% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
